pattern: Placing the most frequent character first into alternating slots, or repeatedly emitting the two highest-count characters, spreads duplicates so no two neighbours match when it is feasible at all.
hints: It is impossible exactly when one character appears more than half the rounded-up length of the string.
Always extend the answer with the currently most frequent remaining character that differs from the last one placed.

Use a max-heap keyed by remaining count: pop the top, append it, and hold it aside until after the next pop so it cannot be placed twice in a row; if you ever cannot place anyone, return the empty string.

edge_inputs.description: two of one letter and one of another can interleave
one letter dominates so it is impossible
all identical of length two is impossible
just over the threshold is impossible

stress.description: random lowercase strings over a small alphabet so feasible and infeasible cases both appear
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    alpha = string.ascii_lowercase[:rng.randint(1, 6)]
    return [''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))]
stress.note: small alphabet biases toward high-frequency characters, exercising the impossible branch
